title: andropogon halepensis subsp. sudanensis
intro: Andropogon halepensis subsp. sudanensis is a sub-species of the Dhaman grass, a type of perennial grass native to Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Andropogon halepensis subsp. sudanensis include its ability to reduce fever, treat digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, and are often used to soothe coughs and colds, as well as to promote wound healing.
